response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable, senior full-stack and distributed-systems engineer with strong craftsmanship values, but he does not meet the minimum qualifications for this role. The job requires 8 years of C++ experience as a hard floor; the resume shows roughly 6 months of C/C++ work at National Instruments and a bachelor's thesis project — well short of the bar. His primary stack is TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. While he has genuine large-scale distributed-systems exposure at Sociomantic and some cloud tooling (AWS CDK) at Meta, there is no evidence of owning or leading a cloud compute platform, and his leadership roles have been informal rather than explicit technical-lead positions.
strengths: Hands-on large-scale distributed systems experience: Sociomantic micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests per second demonstrates real production infrastructure breadth
Strong software-craftsmanship culture: consistent TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and active mentoring through workshops and refactoring dojos
Current Staff Engineer at Google signals high engineering calibre and exposure to complex, cross-functional AI-agent and developer-tooling work
concerns: Critical C++ gap: estimated 6–12 months of C/C++ experience versus the 8-year minimum requirement — the single largest disqualifier
No cloud compute platform ownership: experience is web/application-layer and tooling-focused; no evidence of leading infrastructure strategy at the compute, storage, or hardware-architecture level
Informal leadership only: no explicit technical-lead title or documented responsibility for team roadmaps, headcount, or cross-team architectural decisions
